title: Studio Zero Audit Output v1
description: Stable contract for every audit run's terminal output. Produced by Jury synthesis at the `verdict_emitted` state of the run state machine (ia/user-flows/audit-run-state-machine.md). Consumed by the web app, score engine, score_snapshots persistence, share view at /v/<short-id>, and the verdict-card component. PRD §9.4. Rounding mode and verdict thresholds are owned by score_engine.v1.json — this schema only constrains shape and integrity.

verdict.description: Terminal verdict per PRD §10. Renders as the verdict-card <h1 role="status"> text. Halo HC1: never communicated by color alone — text + icon + color triple.

score.description: Deterministic readiness score per PRD §10 formula `max(0, round_half_even(100 - Σ severity_weight[finding.severity]))`. Integer 0..100 after rounding. Reproducible from `findings` + the `score_engine_version` referenced — re-running the score engine on the same findings array MUST yield this exact integer.

score_engine_version.description: Pinned score engine version that produced `score` and the verdict thresholds. Never edited in place — new rubric = new version (v2, v3…). Persisted to score_snapshots row alongside the integer score so re-audits across version boundaries can be compared apples-to-apples via the analytics-emitted v1_equivalent_score column (closes Atlas v0.2 Critical 1).

audience.description: Free-text audience label captured at intake (PRD §5 personas) and echoed back so the verdict card subhead and share-view social card render the same audience phrasing.

watermark.description: PRD §17 D7. `"private-run-self-audited"` when the run was produced by a local (CLI-mode) runner; `null` for hosted-runner BYOK + Managed. The verdict-card renders the Private Run · Self-Audited chip + aria-describedby help-text iff this is non-null.

findings.description: Every issue discovered by the audit, ordered most-severe first. Empty array is permitted (PASS at 100).

score_breakdown.description: Per-category 0..100 sub-scores. Computed from findings tagged with the matching reviewer (PRD §10 + §9.4). Powers the radar chart + semantic <table> on verdict-card (Halo HC3 — SC 1.1.1, SC 1.3.1, SC 1.4.11).

partial.description: Optional. `true` when this verdict was emitted by the `partial_completed` terminal state (audit-run-state-machine.md). Excluded from the §15 first-audit-FAIL-rate SLI denominator. Absent or false on a complete run.

run_id.description: Optional. Stable ULID of the run that produced this output. Echoed for share-view + verdict-card support-context surface. When absent, the wrapping runs row is the authority.

generated_at.description: Optional. RFC 3339 timestamp of Jury synthesis completion. Distinct from runs.completed_at which is set by the DB row update; this is the runner's witness.

score_engine_provenance.description: Optional but recommended. Cryptographic provenance of the score engine that produced the score: sha256 of the score_engine.v<n>.json file embedded in the runner binary at build time. Lets re-audits prove the rubric hasn't drifted unilaterally.

id.description: Stable per-run finding id. Format `F-NNN` zero-padded to 3+ digits. Referenced by score_snapshots.finding_ids and by the Auto-PR commit trailer at V1.5 (PRD §11.2).

reviewer.description: Which audit-layer agent produced this finding. Drives score_breakdown bucketing and the verdict-card filter-by-reviewer affordance.

severity.description: Strict-elite rubric per PRD §10. Weights live in score_engine.v1.json — never duplicate them in the runner.

layer.description: Which system-architecture layer the finding lives in. Used by the FindingsList group-by-file/layer affordance.

summary.description: One-line human-readable summary. Renders as the finding row title in the verdict-card FindingsList.

recommendation.description: Fix prose + code snippet. MVP tier ships specs-only (PRD §11.1); V1.5 Auto-PR consumes this field to compose commits.

estimated_effort.description: T-shirt sizing. Drives the V1.5 Auto-PR tier pricing (D5 — open). Also surfaces on the FindingsList sort affordance.

wcag_sc.description: Optional. Only valid when reviewer = 'halo'. WCAG 2.2 success criterion(s) cited, e.g. ['1.1.1', '2.5.8']. Renders on the evidence viewer.

ai_disclosure.description: EU AI Act Art. 50 disclosure for AI-authored recommendation snippets that the customer may publish (PRD §11.3). Header `X-AI-Generated: studio-zero` is emitted at HTTP layer; this object captures the per-finding provenance.

evidence.description: Proof material for the finding. Discriminated on `type`. Screenshot evidence MUST carry `alt` (Halo HC4 — SC 1.1.1). Transcripts MUST be rendered as semantic DOM text by the consumer (SC 1.4.5) — this schema enforces shape, the UI enforces rendering.

path.description: Repo-relative POSIX path. Never an absolute path — leaks runner FS layout.

snippet.description: Inline code excerpt. Per Atlas v0.2 Minor (5KB boundary): if the snippet exceeds 5120 bytes, the runner MUST instead use a storage_path reference (see object-evidence variant below) to avoid table bloat in findings.evidence JSONB.

oneOf.title: screenshot evidence (alt REQUIRED per Halo HC4 / SC 1.1.1)

storage_path.description: Supabase Storage object key under the per-tenant evidence bucket. Never a public URL — viewer mints a short-lived signed URL.

alt.description: REQUIRED. Halo HC4 — WCAG SC 1.1.1 Non-text Content. Describes the screenshot's meaningful content for assistive tech and the verdict-card share view. Must not be empty even if the screenshot is decorative; if truly decorative, do not ship it as evidence.

wcag_sc.description: WCAG 2.2 SC(s) the screenshot evidences, e.g. SC 1.4.3 (contrast), SC 2.5.8 (target size 24×24 CSS px).

oneOf.title: transcript evidence (must render as semantic DOM, SC 1.4.5)

transcript.description: Screen-reader or other transcript content. UI surface MUST render as semantic DOM text (Halo HC4) — never as opaque JSON in a <code> block.
